# Session Log - 2026-01-27

## Development Transcript

### Resume Session

**User:** Ran `/q-resume-session`

**Claude:** Found RESUME-STATE.md from Session 130. Displayed saved state (PageSpec v1.3 upgrade, next steps: rewrite PAGES-MAP.md as auto-generated index). Read PLAN.md, checked RFC/INDEX.md (CD-035 open, 0/30 items). User confirmed to continue from saved state. Deleted RESUME-STATE.md.

### Auto-Generate PAGES-MAP.md

**User:** Confirmed resuming from saved state (rewrite PAGES-MAP.md)

**Claude:** Created `scripts/generate-pages-map.ts` — reads all 66 page JSONs and generates PAGES-MAP.md with two lookup tables (by code grouped by section, by route alphabetical). Fixed 3 issues on first run: removed LEAD from on-hold list (was incorrectly flagged by P3 priority heuristic, switched to explicit code list only), added SBOK section override (JSON in courses/ but logically Session), fixed 2 admin JSONs with `"title": "Unknown"` (certificates.json, sessions.json). Added npm scripts `pages-map` and `pages-map:write`. Updated CLAUDE.md references to note auto-generation.

### Explore Site Map Options

**User:** "Let's explore making a sitemap for this site"

**Claude:** Entered plan mode. Explored codebase: no @astrojs/sitemap installed, no `site` config in astro.config.mjs, robots.txt already references peerloop.com/sitemap.xml but no sitemap exists. Designed plan for custom SSR endpoint at `src/pages/sitemap.xml.ts`.

**User:** Clarified: wants comprehensive (all pages), static patterns only (no D1 enumeration)

**Claude:** Updated plan for comprehensive sitemap with static routes. Presented plan for approval.

### Pivot to Interconnection Visualization

**User:** "I want to see the sitemap rather than implement a sitemap. The current routing is subject to change. I need a way to see how pages interconnect."

**Claude:** Pivoted from SEO sitemap to developer-facing visualization. Asked about output format.

**User:** Chose Mermaid diagram

**Claude:** Created `scripts/generate-site-map.ts` — reads page JSON `connections.outgoing` data, generates Mermaid flowchart with labeled edges (trigger descriptions), pages grouped into subgraphs by section. Generated SITE-MAP.md: 60 pages, 202 connections.

### Focus Area Diagrams

**User:** "I think it is too big for usefulness. Can you recommend a way to divide it up?"

**Claude:** Recommended `--focus` flag with 5 role-based areas (public, student, teacher, creator, admin). Each shows primary pages + boundary nodes with dashed borders.

**User:** Approved approach

**Claude:** Implemented `--focus <area>` flag. Initial version had too many boundary nodes (26 for student) because bidirectional edge filter pulled in every page linking INTO the focus area. Changed to outbound-only edges — boundary nodes dropped to 6-11 per focus. Final output: 5 Mermaid diagrams in SITE-MAP.md, each with connections table. Added npm scripts `site-map` and `site-map:write`.

### End Session

**User:** Ran `/q-end-session`

**Claude:** Ran code quality checks (smart mode: TS pre-existing errors, ESLint pass, Tailwind/Astro skipped). Created session files.
